problem.statement: Developers building on Google Sheets, Airtable, or Notion as lightweight backends must implement three separate OAuth flows, three incompatible record schemas, and three pagination models — and rewrite every data access call when a client switches platforms.

evidence.summary: Three Sheets-only API products each report 10K–50K developer accounts paying $9–$49/mo — proving strong WTP for a managed Sheets REST API with no free tier.
50+ threads requesting higher rate limits and better SDK ergonomics; Airtable's hard 5 req/s limit is a known pain point with no paid workaround.
4,000+ stars on the official JS SDK; unofficial wrappers exist precisely because the official API DX for relational data requires multiple nested calls per record set.

existing_solutions.name: SheetDB
existing_solutions.weakness: Google Sheets only; pay-per-request at higher tiers

existing_solutions.name: Notion native API
existing_solutions.weakness: Complex cursor pagination; requires multiple nested calls for relational data

primary.description: Freelance developer or indie hacker building client portals, internal tools, or SaaS prototypes on top of spreadsheet/database platforms.
primary.job_to_be_done: When I'm building an app on top of Google Sheets, Airtable, or Notion / I want a single REST API that normalizes all three platforms into one consistent interface / so I can ship faster and switch backends without rewriting my data layer.
primary.willingness_to_pay_notes: $19–$49/mo validated by SheetDB's thousands of paying customers at the same price range for a Sheets-only product with less capability.

distribution.notes: Primary discovery channel for API consumers. List with normalized endpoint examples and pricing tiers.
Launch post targeting developers and indie hackers. Lead with the multi-platform unified schema angle.
Show HN post. Technical framing — focus on rate-limit buffering and normalized schema moat.
Tutorial article: 'I built a unified Sheets/Airtable/Notion API — here's how'. Include curl examples from /docs.

revenue_target.note: Conservative first-month target: 10 Starter customers × $19/mo = $190/mo MRR. Calculated as lowest_tier_price × 10.

core_flow: Receives normalized {id, source, fields, created_at, updated_at} records
Calls POST /v1/records to create a record

in.name: Normalized records CRUD
in.description: GET /v1/records (list + filter + sort + cursor pagination), GET /v1/records/:id, POST, PATCH, DELETE
in.rationale: Core of the job-to-be-done: one REST interface regardless of source

in.name: API key auth
in.description: Bearer token auth middleware; demo key for evaluation
in.rationale: Required to ship any paid tier — every call must be attributed to a project

in.name: Interactive docs
in.description: /docs page with live try-it form and curl examples; /openapi.json
in.rationale: Self-serve onboarding — developer must be able to evaluate without contacting anyone

in.name: Connected sources list
in.description: GET /v1/sources returns all connected data sources for the authenticated project
in.rationale: Necessary for multi-source workflows and for the dashboard onboarding flow

in.name: Health endpoint
in.description: GET /health returns {status: ok} for uptime monitoring and Replit deploy verification
in.rationale: Non-negotiable for any hosted API product

out.name: Google Sheets OAuth connect flow
out.reason: Not implemented in current build — data layer is mock-only

out.name: Airtable integration
out.reason: v0.2 scope per research; rate-limit buffering is a separate engineering effort

out.name: Usage counter / SQLite metering
out.reason: Not implemented; required for tier enforcement

out.name: Rate limiting (200/2000 req/min)
out.reason: Not implemented; required before public launch

out.name: Per-project API key generation
out.reason: Current build uses single hardcoded demo key

additions: Stripe Checkout integration wired to Starter ($19/mo) and Pro ($49/mo) price points
Stripe webhook handler: checkout.session.completed → activate plan, customer.subscription.deleted → downgrade to free
Per-project API key generation and storage (SQLite)
Usage metering in SQLite: calls/day per API key, enforced against tier limits
First-run onboarding flow: connect Google Sheets via OAuth, receive project API key
Empty states on /docs: 'No sources connected yet — connect Google Sheets to get started'
Error states: 401 (bad key), 429 (rate limit), 503 (source unavailable), 404 (record not found)
Landing page copy (index.html): problem → solution → pricing → CTA
RapidAPI listing: endpoint reference, normalized schema example, pricing tiers
Hacker News Show HN post draft
Deployment checklist with env_vars, webhook URL pattern, Replit verification
non_negotiables: Stripe Checkout completes end-to-end: test card → webhook → plan activated → API key unlocked
Rate limit enforced: free key returns 429 after 200 req/min, paid key allows 2000 req/min
Usage counter persists across server restarts (SQLite, not in-memory)
GET /health returns 200 after all mmp additions
All distribution assets written to /marketing/ directory with correct filenames
Deployment checklist includes STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, DATABASE_URL
PORT binding uses process.env.PORT, server binds to 0.0.0.0

stack.auth: API key (Bearer token); Google OAuth for source connect

env_vars.description: Port for Express to listen on. Defaults to 3000.
Stripe secret key for payment API calls.
Stripe webhook signing secret for verifying webhook payloads.
Google OAuth2 client ID for Sheets connect flow.
Secret for signing session cookies during OAuth flow.
Public base URL of the deployment, used to construct the Google OAuth redirect URI. e.g. https://<repl-name>.<username>.repl.co

external_services.name: Google Sheets API
external_services.purpose: OAuth source connection and record read/write

decision_log.reason: Added SQLite (node:sqlite built-in) for API key storage and usage metering. Switched from better-sqlite3 (requires native compilation) to node:sqlite (zero-dependency, Replit-safe, Node.js 22+).

research_complete.notes: Full research run completed. Slug: spreadsheet-db-bridge-api. Score: 18/25.

scope_approved.notes: Scope approved. MVP targets Google Sheets only (v0.1). Airtable and Notion deferred.

ui_ready.notes: public/index.html and public/docs.html present. Vanilla HTML, no build step.

mvp_functional.notes: Real Google Sheets integration landed: OAuth flow, SQLite persistence (node:sqlite), per-project API key issuance, usage metering, rate limiting. All MVP endpoints verified against live server.

ship_approved.notes: Approved. Real Sheets integration confirmed. Ready for mmp-agent.

mmp_complete.notes: All non-negotiables verified. Distribution assets generated. Stripe checkout wired. Rate limiting enforced. SQLite usage metering persists. GET /health returns 200.

_runs.notes: Full research pipeline run. research.md and mockup-brief.md written to research/2026-03-28-1045/.
Mockup built and pushed to Gabangxa/sheetbridge-api. API shape correct, data layer is mock-only.
